title: Why I'm still hiring senior devs over AI juniors
post:
Hot take: AI coding tools make senior developers more valuable, not less.

Yes, AI can write code fast. But shipping software isn't just about code. It's about understanding trade-offs, navigating ambiguity, making the right architectural calls, and knowing when NOT to build something.

That's not something AI does well yet.

I've been hiring senior engineers who use AI as a multiplier — and the results are incredible. They ship faster, make better decisions, and level up our entire team.

The developers who'll struggle in this environment are juniors relying on AI to close the experience gap. The compounding knowledge that comes from years of solving hard problems still matters.

Am I wrong? Convince me otherwise. Thoughts?
